full_context: Dielectric loss power under AC voltage can be calculated by P = ω C U_rms² tanδ, where ω = 2πf, C is the equivalent capacitance of the test object, U_rms is the RMS value of applied AC voltage, and tanδ is the dielectric loss factor.

variable_definitions.name: Dielectric Loss Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Active power generated by dielectric polarization loss

variable_definitions.name: Angular Frequency
variable_definitions.meaning: 2πf, where f is the supply frequency

variable_definitions.name: Capacitance
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent capacitance of the test object

variable_definitions.name: RMS Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: RMS value of applied AC voltage

variable_definitions.name: Dielectric Loss Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of dielectric loss to capacitive reactive power

potential_questions: Given f=50Hz, C=1μF, U_rms=5kV, tanδ=0.02, calculate P
task_description: 1. Determine frequency f and calculate ω=2πf; 2. Obtain C and U_rms; 3. Obtain tanδ; 4. Calculate P=ω C U_rms² tanδ; 5. Output P

full_context: In DC withstand voltage test, leakage current can be estimated by Ohm’s Law, where R is the converted insulation resistance and U is the applied voltage.
task_description: 1. Convert to obtain R; 2. Take test voltage U; 3. Calculate using I=U/R; 4. Output I

full_context: Calculate the creepage reserve factor for the selected number of insulator units. R>1 means sufficient margin.
task_description: 1. Determine m; 2. Calculate R=(mL_0)/(λU_m); 3. Check whether R>1

full_context: In DC withstand or insulation test, leakage current can be calculated from test voltage and insulation resistance using Ohm’s Law. R is the converted insulation resistance, U is applied DC voltage, and I is leakage current.

variable_definitions.name: Leakage Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Current through insulation under applied voltage

variable_definitions.name: DC Test Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Voltage applied to test object during withstand test

variable_definitions.name: Insulation Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent resistance of test object (converted R2)

potential_questions: Given U=2800V, R=300MΩ, calculate I
task_description: 1. Obtain converted insulation resistance R; 2. Obtain test voltage U; 3. Calculate leakage current using I=U/R; 4. Output I

full_context: Calculate dielectric loss power of insulation under AC voltage. P is dielectric loss power, f is frequency, C is capacitance, U is test voltage RMS, and tanδ is dielectric loss factor.

variable_definitions.name: Dielectric Loss Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Active power consumed by insulation in AC field

variable_definitions.name: Frequency
variable_definitions.meaning: Power supply frequency

variable_definitions.name: Capacitance
variable_definitions.meaning: Test object capacitance

variable_definitions.name: Test Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: RMS value of applied AC voltage

variable_definitions.name: Dielectric Loss Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Loss tangent of insulation (tanδ₂)

potential_questions: Given f=50Hz, C=5000pF, U=10kV, tanδ=0.022, calculate P
task_description: 1. Obtain frequency f, capacitance C, voltage U, dielectric loss factor tanδ; 2. Convert all units to SI; 3. Calculate P = 2πf C U² tanδ; 4. Output P

full_context: Air density decreases at high altitude, reducing external insulation strength. The required withstand voltage must be increased to maintain the same insulation level. This formula calculates the required withstand voltage at high altitude.

variable_definitions.name: Required Withstand Voltage at High Altitude
variable_definitions.meaning: Withstand voltage required for equipment considering altitude correction

variable_definitions.name: Altitude Correction Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Factor correcting external insulation strength with altitude

variable_definitions.name: Standard Withstand Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Withstand voltage under standard conditions at 1000m and below

potential_questions: Given K_a=1.3, U_0=1050kV, calculate U_H
task_description: 1. Obtain altitude correction factor K_a; 2. Obtain standard withstand voltage U_0; 3. Calculate U_H = K_a × U_0; 4. Output U_H

full_context: Winding hotspot temperature equals top-oil temperature plus temperature rise of hotspot over top oil.

variable_definitions.name: Hotspot Temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Absolute temperature of the hottest point in winding

variable_definitions.name: Top-Oil Temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Temperature of transformer top oil

variable_definitions.name: Hotspot Temperature Rise
variable_definitions.meaning: Difference between hotspot temperature and top-oil temperature

potential_questions: Given θ_o=85℃, Δθ_h=83K, calculate θ_h
task_description: 1. Obtain top-oil temperature θ_o; 2. Obtain hotspot temperature rise Δθ_h; 3. Calculate θ_h = θ_o + Δθ_h; 4. Output θ_h

full_context: Calculate tangent value from cosine of power factor angle, used in reactive power compensation calculation.

variable_definitions.name: Tangent of Power Factor Angle
variable_definitions.meaning: Tangent value of power factor angle

variable_definitions.name: Power Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of active power to apparent power

potential_questions: Given cosφ=0.75, calculate tanφ
task_description: 1. Obtain power factor cosφ; 2. Calculate cos²φ; 3. Calculate 1/cos²φ - 1; 4. Take square root to get tanφ; 5. Output tanφ

full_context: Estimate voltage variation caused by injected reactive current I_T across system reactance X. This is a simplified method to evaluate voltage support effect of dynamic reactive power.

variable_definitions.name: Voltage Variation
variable_definitions.meaning: Voltage change at PCC caused by reactive current injection

variable_definitions.name: Injected Dynamic Reactive Current
variable_definitions.meaning: RMS value of dynamic reactive current injected by wind farm

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent System Reactance
variable_definitions.meaning: Short-circuit reactance seen from wind farm PCC

variable_definitions.name: Rated System Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated line voltage at PCC

variable_definitions.name: System Short-Circuit Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Three-phase short-circuit capacity at PCC

potential_questions: Given I_T=990A, U_N=110kV, S_k=5000MVA, calculate ΔU
task_description: 1. Obtain injected dynamic reactive current I_T; 2. Obtain rated voltage U_N and short-circuit capacity S_k; 3. Calculate system reactance X ≈ U_N² / S_k (unify units); 4. Calculate ΔU ≈ (I_T * X) / √3; 5. Output ΔU

full_context: Basic formula for rated current calculation in three-phase AC system, used for reactive current calculation in PV and wind power plants.

variable_definitions.name: Rated Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Output line current under rated condition

variable_definitions.name: Rated Active Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated output active power of equipment

variable_definitions.name: Rated Line Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated line voltage of the system

potential_questions: Given P_N=50MW, U_N=35kV, calculate I_N
task_description: 1. Obtain rated power P_N (convert to W); 2. Obtain rated voltage U_N (convert to V); 3. Calculate √3; 4. Calculate I_N = P_N / (√3 × U_N); 5. Output I_N

full_context: Actual current is obtained by multiplying overcurrent ratio by rated current.

variable_definitions.name: Actual Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual current under overcurrent condition

variable_definitions.name: Overcurrent Ratio
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of actual current to rated current

variable_definitions.name: Rated Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated operating current of equipment

potential_questions: Given I=1.5, I_N=1500A, calculate I_actual
task_description: 1. Obtain overcurrent ratio I; 2. Obtain rated current I_N; 3. Calculate I_actual = I × I_N; 4. Output I_actual

full_context: Compare calculated performance index (e.g., overshoot) with limit required by standard or specification to determine compliance.

variable_definitions.name: Overshoot
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum percentage deviation beyond steady-state value in step response

variable_definitions.name: Overshoot Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum allowable overshoot specified by standard

potential_questions: Given M_p=15%, M_p_limit=30%, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Obtain calculated performance index M_p; 2. Obtain specified limit M_p_limit; 3. Compare M_p and M_p_limit; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Judge whether planned capacitor bank capacity is close to resonant critical capacity. 80% of critical capacity is usually taken as the threshold for significant risk.

variable_definitions.name: Planned Installation Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Proposed capacitor bank capacity

variable_definitions.name: Critical Resonance Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Capacitor capacity that may cause harmonic resonance of specific order

potential_questions: Given Q_plan=150Mvar, Q_cx=200Mvar, determine if significant risk exists
task_description: 1. Obtain planned capacity Q_plan; 2. Obtain critical resonance capacity Q_cx; 3. Calculate 0.8 × Q_cx; 4. Compare Q_plan with 0.8×Q_cx; 5. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Actual allowable temperature rise corrected by cooling medium temperature equals standard temperature rise limit plus adjustment.

variable_definitions.name: Actual Allowable Temperature Rise
variable_definitions.meaning: Corrected allowable temperature rise

variable_definitions.name: Standard Temperature Rise Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Allowable temperature rise at standard cooling medium temperature

variable_definitions.name: Temperature Rise Adjustment
variable_definitions.meaning: Correction value added to standard limit

potential_questions: Given ΔT_std=80K, ΔT=1K, calculate ΔT_actual
task_description: 1. Obtain standard temperature rise limit ΔT_std; 2. Obtain temperature rise adjustment ΔT; 3. Calculate ΔT_actual = ΔT_std + ΔT; 4. Output ΔT_actual

full_context: Verify whether actual total creepage distance of insulator string meets minimum required distance based on pollution level and system voltage.

variable_definitions.name: Actual Creepage Distance
variable_definitions.meaning: Sum of creepage distances of all insulators

variable_definitions.name: Required Specific Creepage Distance
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum unified specific creepage distance by pollution level

variable_definitions.name: Maximum System Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum possible operating line voltage

potential_questions: Given l=3500mm, λ=31mm/kV, U_m=126kV, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Obtain actual creepage distance l; 2. Obtain required specific creepage distance λ and max voltage U_m; 3. Calculate minimum required creepage distance λ × U_m; 4. Compare l with (λ × U_m); 5. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Verify whether design tension of conductor is less than maximum allowable tension to meet mechanical strength safety requirements.

variable_definitions.name: Design Maximum Tension
variable_definitions.meaning: Design tension at lowest point of conductor sag

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Allowable Tension
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum tension conductor can withstand

potential_questions: Given T_design=35kN, T_max=39.28kN, determine if safe
task_description: 1. Obtain design maximum tension T_design; 2. Obtain maximum allowable tension T_max; 3. Compare T_design and T_max; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Compare calculated comprehensive voltage qualification rate with specified target.

variable_definitions.name: Comprehensive Voltage Qualification Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Calculated comprehensive voltage qualification rate

variable_definitions.name: Target Qualification Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Required voltage qualification rate target

potential_questions: Given γ=98.8%, γ_target=98.5%, determine if qualified
task_description: 1. Obtain comprehensive voltage qualification rate γ; 2. Obtain target value γ_target; 3. Compare γ and γ_target; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Compare calculated flicker value caused by load with assigned limit to determine if it exceeds the threshold.

variable_definitions.name: Load Flicker Value
variable_definitions.meaning: Calculated flicker caused by load

variable_definitions.name: Flicker Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum allowable flicker for the load

potential_questions: Given P_lt2=0.72, E_i=0.7, determine if exceeded
task_description: 1. Obtain load flicker value P_lt2; 2. Obtain flicker limit E_i; 3. Compare P_lt2 and E_i; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Verify whether standard conductor cross-section selected by code or ampacity meets minimum cross-section required for thermal stability.

variable_definitions.name: Standard Cross-Section
variable_definitions.meaning: Nominal cross-section selected by code or ampacity

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Thermal Stability Cross-Section
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum cross-section required by thermal stability

potential_questions: Given S_std=70mm², S_min=50mm², determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Obtain standard cross-section S_std; 2. Obtain minimum thermal stability cross-section S_min; 3. Compare S_std and S_min; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Verify whether equivalent distance between conductors meets minimum air gap required under switching overvoltage.

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent Horizontal Distance
variable_definitions.meaning: Converted horizontal distance of triangular arranged conductors

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Air Gap (Switching)
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum distance between conductors under switching overvoltage

potential_questions: Given D_x=5.7m, d_min_sw=2.2m, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Obtain equivalent horizontal distance D_x; 2. Obtain minimum air gap requirement d_min_sw; 3. Compare D_x and d_min_sw; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Compare calculated total failure risk rate with maximum allowable risk limit to judge whether operational risk is acceptable.

variable_definitions.name: Total Failure Risk Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Calculated total risk rate

variable_definitions.name: Risk Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum allowable risk rate

potential_questions: Given R=0.025, R_limit=0.03, determine if acceptable
task_description: 1. Obtain total risk rate R; 2. Obtain risk limit R_limit; 3. Compare R and R_limit; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Compare calculated voltage qualification rate with specified target value.

variable_definitions.name: Voltage Qualification Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Calculated voltage qualification rate percentage

variable_definitions.name: Target Qualification Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Required voltage qualification rate

potential_questions: Given Voltage Qualification Rate=99.5%, Target Value=99.0%, determine if qualified
task_description: 1. Calculate voltage qualification rate; 2. Obtain target qualification rate; 3. Compare both values; 4. Output result (Yes/No)

full_context: Judge whether actual capacitor bank capacity is close to resonant critical capacity. 80% of critical capacity is usually taken as the threshold for significant risk.

variable_definitions.name: Actual Installation Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual installed capacity of capacitor bank

variable_definitions.name: Critical Resonance Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Capacitor capacity that may cause harmonic resonance of specific order

potential_questions: Given Q=200Mvar, Q_cx=250Mvar, determine if significant risk exists
task_description: 1. Calculate critical resonance capacity Q_cx; 2. Obtain actual installation capacity Q; 3. Calculate 0.8 × Q_cx; 4. Compare Q with 0.8×Q_cx; 5. Output result (Yes/No)
